content.problemStatement:
You are given integers AAA and BBB. You want to check if there exists some positive integer NNN such that it has exactly AAA odd divisors and exactly BBB even divisors.

For example, 222 has 111 odd divisor (which is 111), and 111 even divisor (which is 222). 888 has 111 odd divisor (which is 111) and 333 even divisors (which are 2,4,82, 4, 82,4,8).

Is it possible? You only need to print Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No based on whether there exists some integer NNN, you do not need to print the value of NNN.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Yes\text{Yes}Yes if there exists a valid integer NNN and No\text{No}No otherwise.


It is allowed to print each character in either case, i.e. YES\text{YES}YES, yes\text{yes}yes, yES\text{yES}yES will all be accepted as a positive response.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : N=1N = 1N=1 satisfies the conditions.
Test Case 2 : All positive integers are divisible by 111 and hence have at least 111 odd divisor.
Test Case 3 : N=2N = 2N=2.
Test Case 4 : N=8N = 8N=8.
